summary: Muddy audience mix but a strong 1994 set with standout "The Greatest Story Ever Told", Help>Slip>Franklin's and killer Good Lovin'.
review: The audience recording is a bit muddy and leans left in the headphones, yet it captures an energetic 1994 show. Highlights include powerful renditions of "The Greatest Story Ever Told", the Me & My Uncle → Mexicali Blues pairing, a fierce "Ramble on Rose", and a respectable Help>Slip>Franklin's sequence followed by solid Days Between into Good Lovin'. Overall the performance shines despite the imperfect tape quality.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review praises the musical performance heavily while noting a muddy, left‑biased audience mix; the strong show outweighs the recording flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard source described as muddy; this AUD has a slight left‑bias in the balance and overall muddiness, but remains the best available version for the show.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets are praised for strong jams and seamless transitions, with no major flubs reported.
